secondary_archetype: Generalist PM (product strategy ownership with roadmap responsibilities)

seniority.level: Manager / Senior IC (people manager with 6+ years experience)
level_signals: MBA preferred signal of senior-level expectation

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: You have 6+ years of experience, ideally with experience in consulting, strategy & operations, or product management at a tech, retail/ecom, or logistics company
You imagine, develop, and execute on workstreams from start to completion with little oversight
You have a talent for summarizing complex problems, and can translate high-level goals into actionable plans
You are resourceful and can engage other teams and resources to independently figure out answers to nebulous questions
You oscillate easily from strategic considerations to tactical execution
Are a self-starter who is energized by a difficult problem
You display extreme ownership across every workstream while ruthlessly prioritizing what will actually move the needle
You operate comfortably in ambiguous environments while maintaining a strong bias for action
You have experience managing a team
You communicate precisely dependent on audience and level of detail necessary to share
Experience in a complex or high-growth environment required
You are adept at analyzing complex data sets paired with partner team feedback to provide insights and recommendations to our leadership.

scope.surface: Commerce Platform New Verticals — Parcel/fulfillment product strategy and operations
scope.business_model: B2B2C marketplace (merchant partnerships + consumer delivery); logistics/fulfillment new verticals
primary_metrics_inferred: Revenue or volume growth for new verticals
core_responsibilities: Lead a team of ICs on high visibility, cross-functional projects
Develop product strategy to grow a high-potential offering within a high-growth business
Build processes, tools, and connections to achieve growth
Get to the lowest level of detail to scale the business
Work directly with Merchants and across Product, Business Development, Operations, Engineering
Analyze complex data sets and provide insights and recommendations to leadership
Propose new service offerings and build business cases
Design implementation strategies and scale new services across the network

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed ($136K-$200K base) with equity upside
Clear reporting structure stated (Director, Commerce Platform New Verticals S&O)
Role is tied to a specific, named strategic bet (New Verticals / Parcel) — not a vague generalist role
Explicit fill-by date (5/31/2026) suggests active, urgent hiring
Comprehensive benefits package described in detail
red_flags: Location type is entirely unclear — JD does not specify city, remote eligibility, or hybrid policy for this role
NYC-specific Covey AEDT disclosure suggests possible NYC office association but is not confirmed as a role requirement
Wide compensation range ($64K spread) may reflect significant location-based pay differential — candidates in lower-cost markets may land at the bottom
Role scope (Parcel/Commerce Platform) is broad and the team size is unstated, making ownership boundaries unclear

fields_inferred_not_stated: location.type (not stated; marked 'unclear' — JD body does not specify remote, hybrid, or onsite for this role)

scope.business_model (inferred from company context and role description)
seniority.level (inferred from years required, people management requirement, and comp range)
ambiguities: The NYC Covey AEDT disclosure references 'jobs in NYC or Remote Jobs Associated With Office in NYC' — it is unclear whether this role is NYC-based, NYC-associated remote, or located elsewhere entirely.

The JD mentions 'ideally with experience in consulting, strategy & operations, or product management' which uses hedging language ('ideally') but is embedded in a 'We're excited about you because' section that lists otherwise hard requirements — treated as hard given overall framing.
MBA is listed as 'preferred' and classified soft; experience requirements around consulting/S&O/PM are listed as 'ideally' but treated as hard given the section context.
